challenges.title: Basic CSS Grid
challenges.description: Write a CSS rule that targets `.grid` and sets `display` to `grid` and `grid-template-columns` to `1fr 1fr 1fr` to create a three-column layout. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.grid' in css, 'Should target .grid'
assert 'display' in css.lower(), 'Should set display property'
assert 'grid' in css.lower(), 'Should use display: grid'
assert 'grid-template-columns' in css.lower(), 'Should set grid-template-columns'
assert '1fr' in css.lower(), 'Should use fr units for flexible columns'
hints: CSS Grid starts with `display: grid;` on the container.
Use `grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;` for three equal-width columns. `fr` means fraction of available space.

challenges.title: Grid Areas and Gap
challenges.description: Write a CSS rule that targets `.layout` and sets `display` to `grid`, defines `grid-template-areas` with at least two named areas (e.g. `'header header'` and `'sidebar main'`), uses `grid-template-columns` with `fr` units, and sets `gap` to a value like `16px`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.layout' in css, 'Should target .layout'
assert 'display' in css.lower(), 'Should set display property'
assert 'grid' in css.lower(), 'Should use display: grid'
assert 'grid-template-areas' in css.lower(), 'Should define grid-template-areas'
assert 'grid-template-columns' in css.lower(), 'Should set grid-template-columns'
assert 'fr' in css.lower(), 'Should use fr units in columns'
assert 'gap' in css.lower(), 'Should set gap between grid cells'
hints: grid-template-areas uses quoted strings to name regions: `grid-template-areas: 'header header' 'sidebar main';`
The `gap` property (shorthand for row-gap and column-gap) adds spacing between grid cells.
